citations.narrative:
The last documented visit to the kennel by the attending veterinarian was more than one year ago on 04-17-2025.
Annual visits by your veterinarian to assess the premises where all dogs are housed are important for ensuring adequate
husbandry and animal care standards are being maintained.
Regularly scheduled visits, not less than every 12 months, by the attending veterinarian to the premises where animals
are kept, are necessary to assess and ensure adequacy of animal and veterinary care as required under the Animal
Welfare Act.
Correct by July 01, 2026.

Required annual veterinary examinations of adult dogs in the kennel are past due. The most recent documented annual
veterinary exams by the attending veterinarian for all adult dogs in the kennel are dated 04-17-2025.
Annual veterinary exams are important to evaluate the health of all breeding stock and to detect diseases or conditions
that may impact the health and well-being of animals. A complete veterinary examination, from head to tail, of each adult
dog by the attending veterinarian is required not less than once every 12 months. The annual veterinary exam for should
include a record of exam findings dated and be signed by your veterinarian.
Correct by July 01, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.13(a)(1) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The last documented visit to the kennel by the attending veterinarian was more than one year ago on 04-17-2025.  
Annual visits by your veterinarian to assess the premises where all dogs are housed are important for ensuring adequate 
husbandry and animal care standards are being maintained. 
Regularly scheduled visits, not less than every 12 months, by the attending veterinarian to the premises where animals 
are kept, are necessary to assess and ensure adequacy of animal and veterinary care as required under the Animal 
Welfare Act. 
Correct by July 01, 2026. 
3.13(a)(2) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
Required annual veterinary examinations of adult dogs in the kennel are past due. The most recent documented annual 
veterinary exams by the attending veterinarian for all adult dogs in the kennel are dated 04-17-2025.  
Annual veterinary exams are important to evaluate the health of all breeding stock and to detect diseases or conditions 
that may impact the health and well-being of animals. A complete veterinary examination, from head to tail, of each adult 
dog by the attending veterinarian is required not less than once every 12 months. The annual veterinary exam for should 
include a record of exam findings dated and be signed by your veterinarian. 
Correct by July 01, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
